hero.headline: DPU-COL Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #41 University 2025 | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹1,89,400

sections.tldr: DPU-COL Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers manpower planning, compensation management, performance and potential management, labour laws, training and development, organisational design, development and change, strategic HR, and HR audit. NAAC A++ NIRF #41. Total fee ₹1,89,400 with ₹10,000 discount. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. edX certifications bundled. UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
DPU-COL's MBA in Human Resource Management covers both the operational HR layer (manpower planning, compensation management, performance management, labour laws, training and development) and the strategic HR layer (strategic HR, HR audit, organisational design and change management). This breadth makes it one of the more comprehensive HR MBA curricula in the online segment at the NAAC A++ level.

Semester 3 has seven subjects (compared to four in most DPU specs) including a Generic Elective option. This deeper curriculum coverage is notable: manpower planning, compensation management, performance and potential management, labour laws, training and development, and organisational design are each standalone subjects rather than modules within a combined course. Semester 4 adds strategic HR, HR audit, another Generic Elective, and project work.

Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune holds NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 (University 2025). edX certifications are bundled. At ₹1,89,400, the programme is mid-range for a NAAC A++ online MBA.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Human Resource Management specialisation subjects. This spec has more subjects per semester than most DPU specialisations.

subjects.name: Manpower Planning
subjects.desc: Workforce demand and supply forecasting, skills inventory analysis, succession planning frameworks, headcount modelling, and HR planning integration with business strategy.

subjects.name: Compensation Management
subjects.desc: Job evaluation methods (point-factor, ranking), pay structure design, variable pay and incentive frameworks, statutory compliance (PF, ESI, gratuity), and benefits benchmarking.

subjects.name: Performance and Potential Management
subjects.desc: Performance appraisal systems (360-degree, MBO, balanced scorecard), potential assessment frameworks, PIP (performance improvement plan) design, and talent review process.

subjects.name: Labour Laws
subjects.desc: Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, Payment of Wages Act, PF and ESI Acts, Shops and Establishments Act, and new labour code overview (Code on Wages, Industrial Relations Code).

subjects.name: Training and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, learning programme design, e-learning and blended learning approaches, ROI of training evaluation (Kirkpatrick model), and leadership development programmes.

subjects.name: Organisational Design, Development and Change
subjects.desc: Organisation structure types, OD intervention selection, change management models (Kotter, ADKAR), resistance management, and culture transformation programmes.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-I: Digital Marketing or Business English
subjects.desc: Students select one elective. Digital Marketing adds cross-functional marketing skills. Business English strengthens professional communication for HR leadership.

subjects.name: Strategic HR
subjects.desc: HR strategy alignment with business goals, HR scorecard design, employer branding, talent management strategy, and HR transformation roadmaps.

subjects.name: HR Audit
subjects.desc: HR audit methodology, compliance audit checklist, HR process effectiveness assessment, attrition analysis, and HR analytics for audit reporting.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-II: Soft Skills or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Students select one elective. Soft Skills covers leadership, negotiation, and team management. Business Ethics covers corporate governance and ethical HR practices.

subjects.name: Project Work Report
subjects.desc: Industry-linked HR project. Typically a compensation benchmarking study, attrition analysis, HR audit, or training effectiveness evaluation.

whoHires.body:
DPU-COL MBA Human Resource Management graduates enter roles across HR functions in all industries:

**Industries:** IT services, manufacturing, banking and financial services, healthcare, FMCG, retail, consulting (HR advisory), and government enterprises

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Recruitment Executive, Compensation and Benefits Analyst, Training Coordinator, Labour Welfare Officer, HR Operations Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Compensation Manager, HR Business Partner, Learning and Development Manager, Employee Relations Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, Chief People Officer, VP HR, Head of Talent Management, CHRO (mid-market), Head of HR Operations

**Employers:** IT (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Capgemini HR teams), banking (HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank HR), manufacturing (Tata Motors, L&T, Mahindra), FMCG (HUL, Nestle, ITC HR), and consulting (KPMG People Advisory, Deloitte Human Capital).

**Note:** DPU-COL provides placement assistance for all MBA programmes.

skills.body:
The Human Resource Management specialisation builds skills across the full HR lifecycle:

**Technical skills:**
- Compensation Design: job evaluation methodology, pay band construction, variable pay structure (sales incentive, ESOPs), and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, gratuity, professional tax)
- Labour Law: Industrial Disputes Act interpretation, standing orders compliance, new labour code (Code on Wages 2019) implementation roadmap, and statutory filing
- HR Analytics: attrition rate calculation, cost-per-hire tracking, time-to-fill benchmarking, employee engagement score analysis, and HR dashboard design
- Performance Management: goal-setting frameworks (OKR, KRA), appraisal calibration process, talent review and succession planning grid, and PIP documentation

**Applied skills:**
- Training and Development: TNA methodology, learning path design, e-learning content evaluation, and ROI calculation for training programmes
- HR Audit: compliance checklist design, HR process maturity assessment, and attrition root cause analysis

**Soft skills:** Handling employee grievances and disciplinary actions, presenting HR strategy to the board, facilitating change management initiatives, negotiating with labour unions, and managing sensitive employee relations cases.

comparisons.body:
Comparing DPU-COL Online MBA Human Resource Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA HRM:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19 and PwC co-teaching. CU wins on NIRF rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and has seven Sem 3 subjects compared to CU's typical four. DPU's curriculum depth in labour laws, compensation, and HR audit as standalone subjects is notable.

**vs Amrita Online MBA HRM:** Amrita at ₹2,20,000 with NIRF #8 and NAAC A++ is the premium option. DPU saves ₹30,600 with the same NAAC grade. Amrita wins on NIRF rank.

**vs LPU Online MBA HRM:** LPU at approximately ₹2,00,000 with Coursera Plus and placement support. DPU saves ₹10,600 with stronger NAAC (A++ vs A+) and NIRF ranking.

**vs IGNOU Online MBA HRM:** IGNOU at ₹66,000 is the lowest-cost option with NAAC A++. IGNOU's industrial relations depth is strong for manufacturing and government contexts. DPU at ₹1,89,400 offers a more contemporary HR curriculum (HR audit, strategic HR, OD) with placement assistance and edX certifications that IGNOU does not provide.

fees.body:
The DPU-COL Online MBA in Human Resource Management has a total programme fee of ₹1,89,400 for Indian students (USD 3,600 for international students).

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹50,000
- Semester 2: ₹50,000
- Semester 3: ₹45,000
- Semester 4: ₹44,400
- **Total: ₹1,89,400**

**Scholarship:** ₹10,000 off for full payment in one or two transactions within 15 days (effective fee ₹1,79,400).

**Payment options:**
- Zero-cost EMI: from ₹2,500/month directly through DPU-COL (no external NBFC, no interest)
- Semester-wise payments as above
- No application fee

**Bundled:** edX certifications included at no extra cost.

Fees are indicative. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com before payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune (DPU-COL) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #41 University rank. It is valid for government HR officer recruitment, PSU HR positions, banking HR promotions, and private-sector HR management roles. edX certifications supplement the credential. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from DPU-COL Online MBA Human Resource Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Rekha S.
items.liked: Labour Laws module covered the new labour codes alongside existing acts — directly applicable to the compliance overhaul my company was conducting
items.disliked: More HR technology content (HRMS platforms, ATS tools) would modernise the programme for digital-first HR roles
items.body: I work as an HR manager at a manufacturing company. The labour laws, compensation management, and performance management subjects were directly applicable. Having seven subjects in Sem 3 meant the depth was genuinely better than competing programmes I evaluated. NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 were accepted by my employer.

items.name: Vivek N.
items.liked: HR Audit subject was unique — most HR MBA programmes don't cover audit methodology as a standalone course. Directly useful for my HR consulting work
items.disliked: Organisational Design could include more contemporary models — agile org design, squad/tribe structures, and remote workforce design are under-covered
items.body: I work as an HR consultant and the HR audit, strategic HR, and OD subjects gave me the frameworks I use in client engagements. DPU's NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 gave the credential weight when pitching to enterprise clients. The edX certifications added practical depth alongside the MBA theory.

items.name: Asha M.
items.liked: Compensation Management covered PF, ESI, gratuity compliance at an operational level — directly useful for my C&B analyst role at an IT company
items.disliked: More international HRM content would benefit candidates working in MNCs or targeting global mobility roles
items.body: I transitioned from an HR executive to an HR Business Partner role using this MBA. The combination of operational HR subjects (compensation, labour laws, TNA) with strategic subjects (strategic HR, HR audit) gave me the full-stack HR vocabulary. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month made it accessible.

faqs.question: How many subjects does the HRM specialisation have compared to other DPU specs?
faqs.answer: The HRM spec has seven subjects in Semester 3 and four in Semester 4 (including project work and generic elective). Most other DPU specialisations have four Sem 3 subjects and three Sem 4 subjects. The additional depth covers manpower planning, compensation, performance management, labour laws, training, and OD as standalone subjects.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover new Indian labour codes?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Labour Laws module covers both existing acts (Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, PF, ESI) and the new labour code overview (Code on Wages 2019, Industrial Relations Code, Occupational Safety Code, Social Security Code). Candidates in compliance roles will find this dual coverage directly applicable during the labour code transition period.

faqs.question: Is DPU HRM valid for government HR officer and PSU recruitment?
faqs.answer: Yes. DPU-COL's MBA is UGC-DEB approved from a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 university. It satisfies the MBA requirement for government HR officer recruitment (Group B), PSU HR management trainee positions, and banking HR promotions.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,89,400 total (Sem 1: ₹50,000, Sem 2: ₹50,000, Sem 3: ₹45,000, Sem 4: ₹44,400). ₹10,000 scholarship for upfront payment within 15 days. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. No application fee. edX bundled. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com.

faqs.question: What electives are available?
faqs.answer: Sem 3 Generic Elective-I: Digital Marketing or Business English. Sem 4 Generic Elective-II: Soft Skills or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance. Students select one per semester.

chooseIf: You want the deepest HR curriculum among NAAC A++ online MBAs — seven Sem 3 subjects covering compensation, labour laws, OD, and performance management as standalone courses.
Your target roles are HR Manager, HRBP, Compensation Manager, or Labour Welfare Officer where HR depth matters.
₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI and edX certifications fits your budget at NAAC A++ level.
You work in manufacturing or compliance-heavy industries where labour law depth is critical.
consider: NIRF top 20 is your employer's filter — CU (NIRF #19) at ₹1,65,000 or Amrita (NIRF #8) at ₹2,20,000 are stronger on rank.
Your target is HR analytics and HRMS platform skills specifically — those are not covered as standalone subjects here.
You need the most affordable HR MBA — IGNOU at ₹66,000 with industrial relations depth is the lowest-cost NAAC A++ option.
